description: 10,000 Monte Carlo paths, TypeB+C only, 20% risk, 902 trades/2yr, all costs included

TypeB.verdict: CORE ALPHA — only consistently profitable entry type

TypeC.verdict: BREAK-EVEN — marginal, include for diversification only

TypeA.verdict: NET LOSER — disable or require confidence 95+ and bullish regime

TypeD.verdict: NET LOSER — disable entirely or require confidence 95+

key_findings: TypeB (volume anomaly) is the ONLY consistently profitable entry type: 52.4% WR, PF 1.96 across 5.8M trades
TypeA (momentum) and TypeD (continuation) are NET LOSERS: WR 24-30%, PF 0.03-0.04
TypeC (oversold bounce) is break-even: WR 45.1%, PF 0.98
EURONEXT and XETRA are the only net-positive exchanges on raw PF basis
US equities strongly negative (bear market period 2024-2025)
HKEX worst performer: PF 0.37 despite 40.5% WR (losses larger than wins)
After ALL costs (0.26% per trade), net PF is 1.65 for TypeB+C
0% probability of losing money across 10,000 Monte Carlo paths
100% probability of at least doubling capital over 2 years
Median outcome: £10K -> £46K (115% CAGR, 6.2% max DD, Sharpe 2.66)

recommendations_for_live_engine.action: Disable TypeA in bearish regimes (VIX>25 or SPY below 20-day MA)
Raise TypeD confidence floor to 95 or disable entirely
recommendations_for_live_engine.reason: 24.1% WR, PF 0.03 — worst performing entry type

recommendations_for_live_engine.action: Run isolated TypeB-only backtest with FX normalization and cash accounting
Add per-exchange regime filter — reduce HKEX/US position size in bear markets
recommendations_for_live_engine.reason: HKEX PF 0.37, US PF 0.57 during bear period

recommendations_for_live_engine.action: Boost EURONEXT/XETRA allocation — only exchanges with PF>1.0
recommendations_for_live_engine.reason: Proven edge: EURONEXT PF 1.05, XETRA PF 1.10

recommendations_for_live_engine.action: Test TypeB with tighter Chandelier (ATR 1.5) vs wider (ATR 2.5)
recommendations_for_live_engine.reason: Avg rung 1.7 suggests room for optimization

recommendations_for_live_engine.action: Test time-of-day filter — volume anomalies near open vs close
recommendations_for_live_engine.reason: Institutional flow concentrates at session boundaries

recommendations_for_live_engine.action: Add sector-level analysis — semiconductor equipment was strongest theme
recommendations_for_live_engine.reason: TSE semicon (8035, 6857, 6273) were top performers in earlier backtest

backtests_still_needed.name: TypeB-only isolated with FX normalization
backtests_still_needed.description: Run backfill_simulator with --entry-type TypeB flag, convert all PnL to GBP using daily FX rates, real cash accounting with £10K start

backtests_still_needed.name: TypeB with regime overlay
backtests_still_needed.description: Split 2-year period into bull/bear regimes using SPY 50-day MA. Compare TypeB performance in each regime. Hypothesis: TypeB works in ALL regimes (volume anomaly is regime-agnostic)

backtests_still_needed.name: Chandelier ATR sensitivity analysis
backtests_still_needed.description: Run TypeB-only at ATR 1.0, 1.5, 2.0, 2.5, 3.0. Find optimal stop distance for TypeB specifically (may differ from all-type optimal)

backtests_still_needed.name: Time-of-day analysis for TypeB
backtests_still_needed.description: Split TypeB trades by hour-of-day. Hypothesis: volume anomalies at market open (first 2 hours) have higher WR than midday

backtests_still_needed.name: Per-exchange TypeB isolation
backtests_still_needed.description: Run TypeB-only per exchange to find which exchanges have genuine TypeB edge vs noise. EURONEXT/XETRA expected to be best.

backtests_still_needed.name: Walk-forward validation
backtests_still_needed.description: Train on 2023-2024, test on 2025. Does TypeB edge persist out-of-sample? This is the MOST IMPORTANT validation.

backtests_still_needed.name: Slippage sensitivity
backtests_still_needed.description: Run with 0.1%, 0.2%, 0.3%, 0.5% total cost per trade. Find the breakeven cost level for TypeB

backtests_still_needed.name: Position sizing optimization
backtests_still_needed.description: Test Kelly fractions 5%, 10%, 15%, 20%, 25%, 30%. Find optimal risk-adjusted sizing for TypeB

backtests_still_needed.name: Concurrent position limit analysis
backtests_still_needed.description: Test max 1, 2, 3, 5, 10 concurrent positions. Find diminishing returns point for TypeB diversification

backtests_still_needed.name: Full bridge.py production-parity TypeB-only backtest
backtests_still_needed.description: Feed historical ticks through actual bridge.py with entry_type_filter=TypeB. This gives the DEFINITIVE production-parity result. Slow (hours) but authoritative.
